volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Pot of Gold (Section 2.2.223 - Section 2.4.306)

sections.label: Section 2.2.223
sections.original: Neque edepol ego te derisum venio neque derideo ,
sections.translation: By my troth , I neither am come to laugh at you , nor am I laughing at you , nor do I think you deserving of it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.224b
sections.original: Cur igitur poscis meam gnatam tibi ?
sections.translation: Why then do you ask for my daughter for your self ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.225
sections.original: Vt propter me tibi sit melius mihique propter te et tuos .
sections.translation: That through me it may be better for you , and through you and yours for me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.226
sections.original: Venit hoc mihi , Megadóre , in mentem , ted esse hominem divitem ,
sections.translation: This suggests itself to my mind , Megadorus , that you are a wealthy man , a man of rank ; that I likewise am a person , the poorest of the poor ; now , if I should give my daughter in marriage to you , it suggests itself to my mind that you are the ox , and that I am the ass ; when I ’ m yoked to you ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.230
sections.original: ubi onus nequeam ferre pariter , iaceam ego asinus in luto ,
sections.translation: and when I ’ m not able to bear the burden equally with yourself , I , the ass , must lie down in the mire ; you , the ox , would regard me no more than if I had never been born ; and I should both find you unjust , and my own class would laugh at me ; in neither direction should I have a fixed stall , if there should be any separation [*] ; the asses would tear me with their teeth , the oxen would butt at me with their horns .

sections.label: Section 2.2.235
sections.original: hoc magnum est periclum , ab asinis ad boves transcendere .
sections.translation: This is the great hazard , in my passing over from the asses to the oxen .

sections.label: Section 2.2.236
sections.original: Quam ad probos propinquitate proxime te adiunxeris ,
sections.translation: The nearer you can unite yourself in alliance with the virtuous , so much the better . Do you receive this proposal , listen to me , and promise her to me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.238b
sections.original: At nihil est dotis quod dem .
sections.translation: But indeed there is no marriage-portion .

sections.label: Section 2.2.238c
sections.original: Ne duas .
sections.translation: You are to give none ; so long as she comes with good principles , she is sufficiently portioned .

sections.label: Section 2.2.240
sections.original: Eo dico , ne me thensauros repperisse censeas .
sections.translation: I say so for this reason , that you mayn ’ t be supposing that I have found any treasures .

sections.label: Section 2.2.241
sections.original: Novi , ne doceas . desponde .
sections.translation: I know that ; don ’ t enlarge upon it . Promise her to me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.241b
sections.original: Fiat . sed pro Iuppiter ,
sections.translation: So be it . ( Starts and looks about .) But , O Jupiter , am I not utterly undone ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.242b
sections.original: Quíd tibi est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.242c
sections.original: Quid crepuit quasi ferrum modo ?—
sections.translation: What was it sounded just now as though it were iron ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.243
sections.original: Hic apud me hortúm confodere iussi . sed ubi hic est homo ?
sections.translation: Here at my place , I ordered them to dig up the garden . ( EUCLIO runs off into his house .) But where is this man ? He ’ s off , and he hasn ’ t fully answered me ; he treats me with contempt .

sections.label: Section 2.2.246
sections.original: quia videt me suam amicitiam velle : more hominum facit ;
sections.translation: Because he sees that I wish for his friendship , he acts after the manner of mankind . For if a wealthy person goes to ask a favour of a poorer one , the poor man is afraid to treat with him ; through his apprehension he hurts his own interest . The same person , when this opportunity is lost , too late , then wishes for it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.250
sections.original: Si hercle ego te non elinguandam dedero usque ab radicibus ,
sections.translation: By the powers , if I don ’ t give you up to have your tongue cut out by the roots , I order and I authorize you to hand me over to any one you please to be incapacitated .

sections.label: Section 2.2.252
sections.original: Video hercle ego te me arbitrari , Eúclio , hominem idoneum ,
sections.translation: By my troth , Euclio , I perceive that you consider me a fit man for you to make sport of in my old age , for no deserts of my own .

sections.label: Section 2.2.254
sections.original: Neque edepol , Megadore , facio , neque , si cupiam , copia est .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , Megadorus , I am not doing so , nor , should I desire it , had I the means [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.255
sections.original: Quid nunc ? etiam mihi despondes filiam ?
sections.translation: How now ? Do you then betroth your daughter to me ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.255b
sections.original: Illis legibus ,
sections.translation: On those terms , and with that portion which I mentioned to you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.256b
sections.original: Sponden ergo ?
sections.translation: Do you promise her then ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.256c
sections.original: Spondeo .
sections.translation: I do promise her .

sections.label: Section 2.2.257
sections.original: Di bene vertant .
sections.translation: May the Gods bestow their blessings on it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.257b
sections.original: Ita di faxint . illud facito ut memineris ,
sections.translation: May the Gods so do . Take you care of this , and remember that we ’ ve agreed , that my daughter is not to bring you any portion .

sections.label: Section 2.2.259
sections.original: Memini .
sections.translation: I remember it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.259b
sections.original: At scio quo vos soleatis pacto perplexarier :
sections.translation: But I understand in what fashion you , of your class , are wont to equivocate ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.260
sections.original: pactum non pactum est , non pactum pactum est , quod vobis lubet .
sections.translation: an agreement is no agreement , no agreement is an agreement , just as it pleases you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.261
sections.original: Nulla controversia mihi tecum erit . sed nuptias
sections.translation: I ’ ll have no misunderstanding with you . But what reason is there why we shouldn ’ t have the nuptials this day ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.262b
sections.original: Immo edepol optuma .
sections.translation: Why , by my troth , there is very good reason for them .

sections.label: Section 2.2.263
sections.original: Ibo igitur , parabo . numquid me vis ?
sections.translation: I ’ ll go , then , and prepare matters . Do you want me in any way ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.263b
sections.original: Istuc . ei et vale .
sections.translation: That shall be done . Fare you well .

sections.label: Section 2.2.264
sections.original: Heus , Pythodice , sequere propere me ad macellum strenue .—
sections.translation: Hallo ! Strobilus , follow me quickly , in all haste , to the fleshmarket .

sections.label: Section 2.2.265
sections.original: Illic hinc abiit . di immortales , obsecro , aurum quid valet .
sections.translation: He has gone hence . Immortal Gods , I do beseech you ! How powerful is gold ! I do believe , now , that he has had some intimation that I ’ ve got a treasure at home ; he ’ s gaping for that ; for the sake of that has he persisted in this alliance .

sections.label: Section 2.3.268
sections.original: ubi tu es , quae deblateravisti iam vicinis omnibus ,
sections.translation: Where are you who have now been blabbing to all my neighbours that I ’ m going to give a portion to my daughter ? Hallo ! Staphyla , I ’ m calling you !

sections.label: Section 2.3.270
sections.original: ecquid audis ? vascula intus pure propera atque elue :
sections.translation: Don ’ t you hear ? Make haste in-doors there , and wash the vessels clean . I ’ ve promised my daughter in marriage ; to-day I shall give her to be married to Megadorus here .

sections.label: Section 2.3.272
sections.original: Di bene vortant . verum ecastor non potest , subitum est nimis .
sections.translation: May the Gods bestow their blessings on it ! But , i ’ faith , it cannot be ; ’ tis too sudden .

sections.label: Section 2.3.273
sections.original: Tace atque abi . curata fac sint cum a foro redeam domum ;
sections.translation: Hold your tongue , and be off . Take care that things are ready when I return home from the Forum , and shut the house up . I shall be here directly .

sections.label: Section 2.3.274b
sections.original: Quid ego nunc agam ?
sections.translation: What now am I to do ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.275
sections.original: nunc nobis prope adest exitium , mi atque erili filiae ,
sections.translation: Now is ruin near at hand for us , both for myself and my master ’ s daughter ; for her disgrace and her delivery are upon the very point of becoming known ; that which even until now has been concealed and kept secret , cannot be so now . I ’ ll go in-doors , that what my master ordered may be done when he comes . But , by my faith , I do fear that I shall have to drink of a mixture of bitterness [*] !

sections.label: Section 2.4.280
sections.original: Postquam óbsonavit erus et conduxit coquos
sections.translation: After my master had bought the provisions , and hired the cooks [*] and these music- girls in the market-place , he ordered me to divide these provisions into two parts .

sections.label: Section 2.4.283
sections.original: Me quidem hercle , dicam tibi palam , non divides ;
sections.translation: By my troth , but you really shan ’ t be dividing me [*] , I tell you plainly . If you wish me to go anywhere whole , I ’ ll do my best .

sections.label: Section 2.4.285
sections.original: Bellum et pudicum vero prostibulum popli .
sections.translation: A very pretty and modest fellow , indeed [*] . As if , when you are a conger by name , you wouldn ’ t like to be cut into pieces .

sections.label: Section 2.4.287
sections.original: Atque ego istuc , Anthrax , alio vorsum dixeram ,
sections.translation: But , Anthrax , I said that in another sense , and not in the one which you are pretending .

sections.label: Section 2.4.289
sections.original: meus hodie faciet .
sections.translation: Now my master ’ s going to be married to-day . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.4.289b
sections.original: Cuius ducit filiam ?
sections.translation: Whose daughter is he to marry ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.290
sections.original: Vicini huius Euclionis senis e proximo .
sections.translation: The daughter of this Euclio , his near neighbour here . For that reason he has ordered half of these provisions here to be presented to him — one cook , and one music-girl likewise .

sections.label: Section 2.4.293
sections.original: Nempe huc dimidium dicis , dimidium domum ?
sections.translation: That is , you take one half to him , the other half home ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.294
sections.original: Nempe sícut dicis .
sections.translation: ’ Tis just as you say .

sections.label: Section 2.4.294b
sections.original: Quíd ? hic non poterat de suo
sections.translation: How ’ s that ? Couldn ’ t this old fellow provide from his own resources for the wedding of his daughter .

sections.label: Section 2.4.296
sections.original: Vah .
sections.translation: Pshaw !

sections.label: Section 2.4.296b
sections.original: Quid negotist ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.296c
sections.original: Quid negoti sit rogas ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter , do you ask ? A pumice stone isn ’ t so dry as is this old fellow .

sections.label: Section 2.4.298
sections.original: Ain tandem ?
sections.translation: Do you really say that it is as you affirm ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.301
sections.original: de suo tigillo fumus si qua exit foras .
sections.translation: if the smoke by chance escapes out of doors through the rafters of his house . Why , when he goes to sleep , he ties a bag [*] beneath his gullet .

sections.label: Section 2.4.303
sections.original: Cur ?
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.303b
sections.original: Ne quid animae forte amittat dormiens .
sections.translation: That when he sleeps , he may lose no breath .

sections.label: Section 2.4.304
sections.original: Etiamne obturat inferiorem gutturem ,
sections.translation: And does he stop up the lower part of his windpipe [*] as well ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.305
sections.original: ne quid animai forte amittat dormiens ?
sections.translation: lest , perchance , he should lose any breath as he sleeps ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.306
sections.original: Haec mihi te ut tibi med aequom est , credo , credere .
sections.translation: In that ’ tis as fair that you should credit me , as it is for me to credit you .
